title: Object basics
description: Keys, values, and how to access them.

questions.prompt: Which is a valid object literal?
questions.explanation: Keys can be unquoted identifiers or quoted strings. = is a syntax error.
Object.keys returns the own enumerable keys. Two: name, age.

questions.prompt: The shorthand `{ name }` inside an object literal is equivalent to:
questions.explanation: ES6 shorthand: when key and value name match, drop the value.
Bracket notation reads the key from a variable — dot notation can't.

questions.code:
const u = { name: 'Ada' };
console.log('name' in u);
questions.explanation: in checks whether a key exists (own or inherited).
delete removes the property; access returns undefined.
entries yields [key, value] pairs — destructure to iterate.
